flashcards.front: What does CIA stand for in cloud security?
What is the shared responsibility model?
flashcards.back: Cloud provider secures infrastructure, customer secures data/applications
Cloud Access Security Broker - enforces security policies between cloud users and providers

flashcards.front: Purpose of security groups in cloud?
flashcards.back: Act as virtual firewalls controlling inbound/outbound traffic at instance level

flashcards.front: What is DLP in cloud security?
flashcards.back: Data Loss Prevention - monitors/prevents unauthorized data transfer

flashcards.front: Key difference between NACLs and security groups?
flashcards.back: NACLs operate at subnet level (stateless), security groups at instance level (stateful)

flashcards.front: What is SIEM in cloud monitoring?
flashcards.back: Security Information & Event Management - real-time analysis of security alerts
Never trust, always verify - strict identity verification for all users/devices

flashcards.front: Main purpose of encryption in transit?
flashcards.back: Protect data confidentiality during transmission using TLS/SSL protocols
Security measures to prevent breaking out of virtual machine isolation
Protect EU citizens' personal data with proper consent and breach notifications

flashcards.front: Purpose of hashing in integrity checks?
flashcards.back: Create unique digital fingerprint to detect data tampering

flashcards.front: What is a cloud security architecture framework?
flashcards.back: Structured plan combining policies/controls to protect cloud assets
Temporary credentials instead of long-term access keys for better security
